mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.25% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.25% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over three consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce equity allocations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market weakness preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following 3-month PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicates demand destruction and margin compression for equities. This dual signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 300.536 and RSAFS at 696,982 million as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with simultaneous inflation and demand weakness
failure_condition: The joint signal (inflation + sales decline) fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a positive trend when measured over a 3-month rolling window, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as earnings surprises materialize.
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial momentum
failure_condition: Positive 3-month INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 52% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,300 thousand units annualized, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand destruction. This threshold-triggered signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as construction and materials equities face headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,382 thousand units as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when HOUST < 1,300, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 3.7% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously declines, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession probability increases sharply.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.4% and PAYEMS at 155,073 thousand as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: labor market inflection point transitioning from tight to loose conditions
failure_condition: The joint signal (rising unemployment + payroll decline) fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.50 percentage points over a 2-month window, it signals monetary policy pivot toward easing. This regime-conditional signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in tightening cycles, as equity risk premiums compress and growth expectations stabilize.
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening to easing within a restrictive cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances following FEDFUNDS declines of 0.50pp+, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains positive and above 0.5% annualized, indicating sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth remains supported by robust aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,198.091 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2022-11-15
regime_hint: any regime with positive real GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
